workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just got this from Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House -- I need help figuring out renewal risk.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-03-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Michelle (Alpine Ski House)

[00:01:15] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is renewal risk.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:09:00] Michelle (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Michelle (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Michelle (Alpine Ski House): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:20:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-L trap (Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-L failure mode (Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-L -- Over-formal coaching notes when transcript was peer-collaborative
